sn40.9:1.1: … "Here, friends, while I was alone in seclusion, a reflection arose in my mind thus: ‘It is said, "the signless concentration of mind, the signless concentration of mind." What now is the signless concentration of mind?’
sn40.9:2.2:
"Then, friends, it occurred to me: ‘Here,
by nonattention to all signs, a bhikkhu enters and dwells in the signless concentration of mind. This is called the signless concentration of mind.’
sn40.9:2.3: "Then, friends, by nonattention to all signs, I entered and dwelt in the signless concentration of mind. While I dwelt therein my consciousness followed along with signs.
sn40.9:2.4: "Then, friends, the Blessed One came to me by means of spiritual power and said this: ‘Moggallana, Moggallana, do not be negligent, brahmin, regarding the signless concentration of mind. Steady your mind in the signless concentration of mind, unify your mind in the signless concentration of mind, concentrate your mind in the signless concentration of mind.’ Then, on a later occasion, by nonattention to all signs, I entered and dwelt in the signless concentration of mind.
sn40.9:2.5: "If, friends, one speaking rightly could say of anyone: ‘He is a disciple who attained to greatness of direct knowledge with the assistance of the Teacher,’ it is of me that one could rightly say this."
